description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, natural appearance and a mix of gray and beige tones. The surface is uneven, featuring a combination of smooth and jagged areas, with some visible marbling and subtle color transitions. One side of the stone has a noticeable depression or hollow, giving it a slightly concave profile, while the rest of the stone is more convex and solid. The stone's coloration includes darker veins and patches, suggesting mineral inclusions or weathering effects. Its overall form is asymmetrical, with no clear geometric pattern, and the texture appears coarse and unpolished. The stone would be well-suited as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban park, garden, or roadside setting.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped stone with gray and beige marbling, featuring a coarse, unpolished surface and a prominent hollow on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the stone's tall, narrow profile with a mostly convex surface and subtle marbling.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the stone's hollow or depression, with more pronounced dark veining and a concave area.
description_view_2: Here, the stone appears more solid and rounded, with less visible hollow and a smoother marbled surface.
description_view_3: This perspective reveals the stone's angular top and a mix of light and dark marbling, with the hollow partially visible.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural marbling and veining with irregular, mottled patches of gray and beige; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and unpolished, with visible signs of natural weathering and minor erosion, but no heavy lichen or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces; may be partially surrounded by grass or soil.
